---
title: Arc Virtual Cell Atlas
alias: &ALIAS arcvca
href: https://virtualcellatlas.arc-institute.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Arc Virtual Cell Atlas (scBaseCamp) is a large-scale single-cell atlas resource developed by the Arc Institute to enable multimodal virtual cell modeling and biological foundation model development.

## Key Features

- Aggregates tens of millions of single-cell profiles across human tissues and disease states
- Designed to serve as a training and benchmarking resource for AI virtual cell models
- Provides harmonized metadata and standardized count matrices for immediate use
- Supports multimodal data integration including RNA, ATAC, and protein measurements
- Open-access with programmatic download and cloud-native data formats (AnnData/Zarr)

## Use Cases in Research

- Training biological foundation models on large-scale single-cell expression data
- Benchmarking virtual cell simulation models against diverse cell states and perturbations
- Generating in silico perturbation predictions for host response to pathogens
- Building reference embeddings for transfer learning in infectious disease contexts
- Enabling data-efficient AI model fine-tuning using broad cellular diversity

## Getting Started

Visit [Arc Virtual Cell Atlas](https://virtualcellatlas.arc-institute.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
